sourceTitle: A more powerful address bar with AI built in
acquiredVia: yt-dlp+ffmpeg (rung 3 of the motion ladder — native-local Chrome recording was unavailable this session: computer-use browser control is read-only/denied by the user, and the claude-in-chrome extension was not connected)
interaction: Real Chrome desktop: a query is typed and committed in the omnibox on the New Tab page (autocomplete dropdown visible, address bar text mid-entry), the tab favicon shows the native loading spinner as the page navigates, Google's AI Mode results page shows a shimmering gradient loading bar under the query with progressive status text ("Thinking…" → "Loading AI response→" → "Pulling it all together…"), then the answer content resolves: paragraph text fades in first, followed by a row of product-comparison cards populating left-to-right.
note: Closest available real-footage match for the 'type a URL into the omnibox, a loading bar sweeps, the page content loads' story without login or bot-check: it's a search query rather than a raw URL and the destination is an AI Mode results page rather than a plain site, but the omnibox commit, native tab-loading spinner, and the shimmer-bar-to-content-resolve motion idiom are genuine Chrome UI captured from an official Google Chrome channel video, not recreated. Use for: omnibox caret/commit timing, tab spinner behavior, loading-bar shimmer easing, and staggered content-reveal pacing (text-first, then cards).
